creative_brief: Hero image: extreme close-up of a marbled grass-fed steak on butcher paper, a handwritten farm-name tag visible (e.g. 'Rachel's Farm, Kempsey NSW'). Muted earth tones, natural light, no supermarket styling. Below the fold, a simple map graphic showing a short paddock-to-hub line versus a long 2,400km supermarket route. Clean, editorial layout — let the food and the farms do the talking.
headline: You will taste the difference when beef is days old, not weeks
subhead: 100% grass-fed. From named regenerative farms in NSW. Collected fresh from your local hub.
hero_copy:
Pick up a steak at the supermarket and try to find the farm name on the label. You will not. Industry data suggests it may have travelled around 2,400 kilometres and sat in cold storage for up to two or three weeks.

Now picture beef that went from a regenerative paddock in Kempsey to a collection hub in Brookvale within days. That is FarmThru. And you can taste every one of those missing kilometres.

sections.heading: Named farms, not supplier codes
sections.body:
Every product on FarmThru comes from a farm you can look up. Rachel's Farm for 100% grass-fed beef raised on pasture without hormones or feedlots. Bundarra Farm for pork. Collins Wild Caught for wild-caught Alaskan salmon from the South Coast. Farmer Brown for eggs. Paris Creek for carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy.

These are not anonymous suppliers. They are farmers who stake their name on what they produce. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. Animals raised on open pasture, on regenerative land.

sections.heading: How the hub keeps it fresh
sections.body:
FarmThru runs a hub-and-collect model from Brookvale, Monday to Friday. You order online. Your food goes from the farm straight to the hub. You collect when it is ready.

Zero wholesalers in the chain. Fewer steps between paddock and kitchen means the food is fresher and the farmer keeps more of what you pay. It is not a meal kit. It is a proper grocery store built around quality, with on-demand ordering and no lock-in. No middlemen. No wholesalers. Sydney families collect from the Brookvale hub.

sections.heading: Be part of what comes next
sections.body:
FarmThru is launching an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal in mid-2026. You can invest from $50 up to $10,000 per person. A $5 refundable VIP deposit gives you first access, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A.

Early-stage investing carries real risk. You could lose some or all of your investment. Full details will be in the disclosure document on Birchal. We can't promise returns — early-stage investing carries real risk.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
